module-10-oop-quality-project.label: Module 10 OOP Quality Project
module-10-oop-quality-project.summary: Stabilize a small catalog app with tests, import fixes, service helpers, and a clean final report.

project_step_report_service_tests.title: Milestone 2: Test and fix the reporting service

project_step_storage_loader.title: Milestone 3: Load catalog items from fixture data

project_step_debug_imports_state.title: Milestone 4: Debug imports and state updates

project_step_regression_summary.title: Milestone 5: Add a reusable summary service and regression tests

project_step_final_regression.title: Milestone 6: Finish the end-to-end regression workspace

try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.title: Milestone 1: validate the catalog model
try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.prompt: Complete `CatalogItem` in `models/catalog_item.py`. Store `name`, `category`, and `quantity`; reject negative quantities with `ValueError`; and make `is_in_stock()` return whether quantity is greater than zero. Keep the runnable demo in main.py.

help.concept: Model tests should cover stored state, normal behavior, and invalid constructor input.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.starterCode:
# Run this provided model demo.
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4)
print(item.name)
print(item.is_in_stock())

main_py.content:

print(item.is_in_stock())

tests_check_model_py.content: # TODO: add assertions for attributes, stock state, and negative quantity.

main_py.content:

print(item.is_in_stock())

tests_check_model_py.content:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4)
assert item.name == "Notebook"
assert item.is_in_stock() is True
try:
    CatalogItem("Broken", "Office", -1)
    raise AssertionError("negative quantity should fail")
except ValueError:
    pass

try_module_10_oop_quality_project_sketch.solutionCode:
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

item = CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4)
print(item.name)
print(item.is_in_stock())

1.message: Report zero quantity as out of stock.

project_step_report_service_tests.title: Milestone 2: test the reporting service
project_step_report_service_tests.prompt: Complete `total_quantity(items)` and `low_stock_names(items, threshold)` in `services/reporting.py`. Return item names at or below the threshold in their original order. Keep the supplied model and main demo working.
project_step_report_service_tests.hint: Use item.quantity for the total and a list comprehension for low-stock names.

help.concept: Reporting functions should return deterministic values that tests can compare exactly.
help.hint_1: Use item.quantity for the total and a list comprehension for low-stock names.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

project_step_report_service_tests.starterCode:
# Run this provided reporting demo.
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem
from services.reporting import total_quantity, low_stock_names

items = [
    CatalogItem("Notebook", "Office", 4),
    CatalogItem("Marker", "Office", 0),
    CatalogItem("Tape", "Office", 2),
]
print(total_quantity(items))
print(", ".join(low_stock_names(items, 2)))

project_step_storage_loader.title: Milestone 3: load catalog items from CSV
project_step_storage_loader.prompt: Complete `load_items(path)` in `storage/catalog_loader.py`. Read the CSV with `csv.DictReader`, convert quantity to int, create `CatalogItem` objects, and return them in file order.
project_step_storage_loader.hint: Loop over DictReader rows and append a CatalogItem for each row.

help.concept: A storage layer converts text rows into validated domain objects.
help.hint_1: Loop over DictReader rows and append a CatalogItem for each row.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

def load_items(path):
    # TODO: read the CSV rows and return CatalogItem objects.
    pass

tests_check_loader_py.content: # TODO: assert the loaded names and quantities.

storage_catalog_loader_py.content:
import csv
from models.catalog_item import CatalogItem

def load_items(path):
    items = []
    with open(path, newline="") as file:
        for row in csv.DictReader(file):
            items.append(CatalogItem(row["name"], row["category"], int(row["quantity"])))
    return items

tests_check_loader_py.content:
from storage.catalog_loader import load_items

items = load_items("data/catalog.csv")
assert [item.name for item in items] == ["Notebook", "Marker", "Tape"]
assert [item.quantity for item in items] == [4, 0, 2]

0.message: Load all three CSV rows as CatalogItem objects.

project_step_debug_imports_state.title: Milestone 4: validate restock state changes
project_step_debug_imports_state.prompt: Finish `CatalogItem.add_stock(amount)` in `models/catalog_item.py`. Reject negative amounts with `ValueError`, add valid amounts to quantity, and return the new quantity. The provided main script restocks Marker by 3.
project_step_debug_imports_state.hint: Validate the amount before changing self.quantity.

help.concept: A model should protect its own state instead of relying on callers to validate every change.
help.hint_1: Validate the amount before changing self.quantity.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

project_step_regression_summary.title: Milestone 5: build a reusable regression summary
project_step_regression_summary.prompt: Complete `build_summary(items, threshold)` in `services/reporting.py`. Return a dictionary with `total_quantity`, `low_stock_names`, and `in_stock_count`. The main script loads the fixture and restocks Marker before printing the summary.
project_step_regression_summary.hint: Reuse the two existing reporting helpers and count items whose is_in_stock() returns True.

help.concept: A summary service combines tested model and storage behavior into one stable result.
help.hint_1: Reuse the two existing reporting helpers and count items whose is_in_stock() returns True.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

project_step_regression_summary.starterCode:
# Run this provided end-to-end summary demo.
from storage.catalog_loader import load_items
from services.reporting import build_summary

items = load_items("data/catalog.csv")
next(item for item in items if item.name == "Marker").add_stock(3)
summary = build_summary(items, 2)
print(summary["total_quantity"])
print(", ".join(summary["low_stock_names"]))
print(summary["in_stock_count"])

project_step_final_regression.title: Milestone 6: finish the regression workspace
project_step_final_regression.prompt: Finish the cumulative quality workspace. Keep the validated model, CSV loader, and summary service working; add meaningful assertions in `tests/check_regression.py`; and complete `README.md` with the responsibility of each folder. The runnable demo must print the final summary and confirmation line.
project_step_final_regression.hint: Run the complete flow in the regression test: load, restock, summarize, and assert.

help.concept: A production-ready handoff includes behavior, regression checks, and clear project documentation.
help.hint_1: Run the complete flow in the regression test: load, restock, summarize, and assert.
help.hint_2: Check the named file and compare your code with each requirement in the prompt.

README_md.content:
# Catalog Quality Project

- `models/` owns validated catalog state.
- `services/` builds reports.
- `storage/` loads CSV data.
- `tests/` protects the full workflow.
- `main.py` demonstrates the final result.

module-10-oop-quality-project-sketch0.title: Project brief: stabilize a catalog app before handoff
module-10-oop-quality-project-sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
A teammate built a small catalog app, but the team does not trust it yet. Some methods work only for the happy path, some imports are fragile, and the report code is too hard to test.

Your job is a **quality pass**, not a total rewrite. You will keep the app recognizable while making it safer.

You will practice the Module 10 skills in a real workflow:

1. Add small assertion-based tests for model behavior.
2. Fix state and import bugs that the tests reveal.
3. Move messy report logic into a service.
4. Add a final regression check so future changes do not break the app silently.

The lesson examples use smaller objects so the ideas are easy to see. This project applies those ideas to a catalog app that feels closer to a real code handoff.
